items.subject: physics
items.topic: how light refraction produces a rainbow
items.transcript: Light refraction is the bending of light as it passes from one medium into another, and it's the key to understanding one of the sky's most familiar sights: the rainbow. When sunlight enters a raindrop, it slows down and bends, because water is denser than air. But here's the important part. White sunlight isn't a single color. It's a mixture of all the colors of the spectrum, and each color bends by a slightly different amount. Red light bends the least, and violet bends the most. So as the light enters the droplet, the colors begin to separate. Now follow the path. The light travels to the back of the raindrop, reflects off the inside surface, and then exits through the front, bending a second time on its way out. By the time it leaves, the colors have spread into a fan. That's why we see distinct bands rather than a blur of white. The reason a rainbow forms an arc has to do with geometry. Each color leaves the droplet at a fixed angle, about forty-two degrees for red and forty degrees for violet, measured from the line between the sun and your eye. Only droplets sitting at exactly that angle send their color to you, and those droplets trace out a circle in the sky. So a rainbow isn't really an object hanging in the air. It's a collection of light from millions of separate droplets, each contributing a single point of color. Move your position, and you see a completely different rainbow, made from a different set of drops.

questions.stem: What is the lecture mainly about?

options.A: How refraction in raindrops separates light into a rainbow
options.B: Why water droplets are denser than the surrounding air
options.C: How reflection off mirrors produces colored light patterns
options.D: The history of scientific theories about sunlight and color

questions.explanation: 正确答案A概括了整堂课的核心：光在雨滴中折射并分离成彩虹。B只是讲解中提到的一个细节（水比空气密），属于too_narrow。C把内部反射偷换成镜面反射，是wrong_topic。D谈论科学史，课中根本没有，是surface_word_trap。

questions.stem: Why does the professor say that white sunlight is a mixture of all colors?

options.A: To explain why water bends light more than air does
options.B: To explain why the separated colors form distinct bands
options.C: To prove that violet light travels faster than red light
options.D: To describe how raindrops reflect light internally

questions.explanation: 正确答案B：教授指出白光由多种颜色组成，是为了说明各颜色弯曲程度不同、最终分散成清晰的色带。A把目的弄错，混淆了密度问题，是wrong_purpose。C宣称紫光更快，课中并未表达，是over_inference。D只是复述了讲座中“内部反射”这一内容，而非解释这句话的目的，是literal_restatement。

questions.stem: According to the professor, how does light behave inside a single raindrop?

options.A: It passes straight through without changing direction
options.B: It bends once on entering but not on leaving
options.C: It enters, reflects off the back, and bends again exiting
options.D: It is absorbed and re-emitted as a single color

questions.explanation: 正确答案C精确复述了讲座描述的路径：进入、在后壁反射、再从前面折射射出。A说光不改变方向，与折射相矛盾，是reversed_logic。B说只弯曲一次，与“出射时第二次弯曲”相矛盾，是wrong_detail。D说被吸收后重新发出单色光，课中并无此说法，是topic_adjacent。

questions.stem: What does the professor imply about two people standing in different spots?

options.A: They cannot see any rainbow unless the sun moves
options.B: They see the same rainbow from the same raindrops
options.C: They see violet light at exactly forty-two degrees
options.D: They each see a rainbow made from different droplets

questions.explanation: 正确答案D：教授说改变位置就会看到由不同雨滴组成的另一道彩虹，故不同位置的人看的是不同雨滴的光。A说太阳不动就看不到彩虹，过度推断，是over_generalization。B说他们看同一道彩虹，与“移动就看到不同彩虹”相反，是reversed_logic。C把红光的四十二度错配给紫光（紫光约四十度），是topic_adjacent。

items.subject: social_psychology
items.topic: how social facilitation affects performance on simple vs complex tasks
items.transcript: Imagine you've practiced a song until you can play it in your sleep. Now imagine playing it in front of a packed auditorium. For many people, the crowd makes them play even better. But ask them to perform a piece they only half-learned, and the same audience makes them fall apart. This pattern has a name in social psychology: social facilitation. The term refers to the way the mere presence of other people changes how we perform a task. Early researchers noticed that cyclists rode faster when racing alongside others than when riding alone against the clock. At first, this looked simple: an audience boosts performance. But later studies complicated the picture. Robert Zajonc proposed an elegant explanation. The presence of others, he argued, raises our level of physiological arousal. And arousal strengthens whatever response is most dominant, most automatic, for us in that moment. For a well-practiced task, the dominant response is the correct one, so an audience helps. For a difficult or unfamiliar task, the dominant response is often a mistake, so an audience hurts. This is why a skilled musician shines on stage while a beginner stumbles. The key variable isn't the audience itself. It's how well-learned the task already is. Researchers later confirmed this even in cockroaches, which ran a simple maze faster with other roaches watching but a complex maze slower. So the next time you choke under pressure, remember that arousal isn't your enemy. It simply amplifies what's already there. The real lesson is about preparation. Practice a skill until the right response becomes the automatic one, and an audience will work in your favor rather than against you.

questions.stem: What is the lecture mainly about?

options.A: Why some musicians prefer to perform alone rather than in groups
options.B: How the presence of others affects easy versus hard tasks
options.C: The physical effects of arousal on the human nervous system
options.D: How cockroaches navigate mazes in laboratory experiments

questions.explanation: 正确答案B抓住主旨：他人在场如何影响简单任务与复杂任务的表现。A关注音乐家偏好，课中并未讨论，是wrong_topic。C只涉及讲座中提到的生理唤醒这一点，是too_narrow。D只是一个佐证实验的细节，被当成主题就属于surface_word_trap。

questions.stem: According to the professor, what did Zajonc propose about the presence of others?

options.A: It always improves performance regardless of the task
options.B: It teaches people the correct response to a new task
options.C: It raises arousal, which strengthens the dominant response
options.D: It lowers arousal so people make fewer mistakes

questions.explanation: 正确答案C准确复述Zajonc的观点：他人在场提高唤醒水平，从而强化最主导的反应。A说总是提升表现，忽略了复杂任务会变差，是wrong_detail。B说在场会教会人正确反应，课中无此说法，是topic_adjacent。D说降低唤醒，与“提高唤醒”相反，是reversed_logic。

questions.stem: Why does the professor mention the cockroaches running mazes?

options.A: To suggest that insects and humans learn in identical ways
options.B: To argue that complex mazes are harder than simple ones
options.C: To describe the equipment used in early cycling studies
options.D: To show the same effect appears even in simple animals

questions.explanation: 正确答案D：蟑螂例子用来说明这一效应在简单动物身上同样出现，从而支持理论的普遍性。A说昆虫与人学习方式完全相同，是教授未表达的over_inference。B只是复述蟑螂实验中复杂迷宫更难这一内容，而非解释提及目的，是literal_restatement。C把蟑螂误说成自行车研究的器材，弄错了用意，是wrong_purpose。

questions.stem: What does the professor imply about performing under pressure?

options.A: Thorough practice makes an audience more likely to help
options.B: Audiences should always be avoided before a performance
options.C: Arousal makes every well-practiced skill worse on stage
options.D: Only professional musicians benefit from having an audience

questions.explanation: 正确答案A：教授强调关键在于把正确反应练成自动反应，这样观众就会起正面作用。B说应永远避开观众，过度推广，是over_generalization。C说唤醒会让熟练技能变差，与讲座观点相反，是reversed_logic。D说只有专业音乐家受益，是讲座未支持的topic_adjacent。

items.subject: art_history
items.topic: the role of patronage in shaping Baroque art
items.transcript: When we admire a dramatic Baroque painting today, we tend to credit the artist alone. But behind almost every major Baroque work stood a patron, and patronage shaped this art far more than we usually acknowledge. In the seventeenth century, artists rarely painted what they personally wished. They painted what a wealthy or powerful client commissioned, and that client set the terms. The Catholic Church was the dominant patron of the era. Following the Council of Trent, the Church wanted art that would stir the emotions of ordinary worshippers and pull them back toward the faith. So painters were asked for vivid drama, intense light, and figures caught in moments of spiritual crisis. Consider Caravaggio. His startling realism and theatrical lighting were not just personal style. They answered a demand for images that felt immediate and overwhelming. Secular patrons mattered too. Absolute monarchs commissioned grand portraits and ceilings designed to project power and glory. The contract often specified the subject, the size, the materials, even the number of figures. An artist who ignored these terms simply did not get paid, or hired again. This doesn't mean patronage erased creativity. Within the constraints, artists found room to innovate, and the best of them turned a client's demands into something genuinely new. But it does mean we misread the period if we imagine the lone genius working in isolation. The emotional intensity we associate with Baroque style was, in large part, a response to what patrons wanted. To understand the art, then, we have to understand the people who paid for it, and the goals those payments were meant to serve.

questions.stem: What is the lecture mainly about?

options.A: The biography and personal struggles of the painter Caravaggio
options.B: How the Council of Trent reformed Catholic religious doctrine
options.C: How patrons' demands shaped the character of Baroque art
options.D: Why secular monarchs preferred portraits over religious scenes

questions.explanation: 正确答案C概括主旨：赞助人的要求如何塑造了巴洛克艺术的特征。A只聚焦Caravaggio个人，是too_narrow。B转向特伦特会议的宗教教义改革，偏离主题，是wrong_topic。D用了课中“世俗君主”“肖像”等词，却造出一个未表达的偏好对比，是surface_word_trap。

questions.stem: According to the professor, what did the Church want from Baroque art after the Council of Trent?

options.A: Calm, restrained images that encouraged quiet reflection
options.B: Abstract designs that avoided depicting human figures
options.C: Accurate historical records of important Church events
options.D: Emotionally stirring images that drew worshippers back

questions.explanation: 正确答案D准确复述：教会想要能打动普通信徒、把他们拉回信仰的情感强烈作品。A说要平静克制的图像，与“激起情感、戏剧性”相反，是reversed_logic。B说避免描绘人物，与课中“处于精神危机中的人物”矛盾，是wrong_detail。C说要历史事件的准确记录，课中无此说法，是topic_adjacent。

questions.stem: According to the professor, what did patrons' contracts often specify?

options.A: The subject, size, materials, and number of figures
options.B: The price the finished work could later be resold for
options.C: The exact emotions each viewer was required to feel
options.D: The other artists the painter was allowed to study under

questions.explanation: 正确答案A直接对应讲座：合同常规定主题、尺寸、材料，甚至人物数量。B说规定转售价格，课中没有，是topic_adjacent。C说规定每位观众必须感受到的情感，把“激起情感”的目的曲解成合同条款，是wrong_detail。D说规定可向哪些画家学习，课中无此内容，是topic_adjacent。

questions.stem: What does the professor imply about the idea of the lone artistic genius?

options.A: It applies perfectly to every Baroque painter of the era
options.B: It gives a misleading picture of how Baroque art was made
options.C: It was invented by patrons to control their commissioned artists
options.D: It explains why Baroque art avoided emotional intensity

questions.explanation: 正确答案B：教授说若想象孤独天才独自创作，就会误读这一时期，暗示这种观念具有误导性。A说它完美适用于每位画家，过度概括，是over_generalization。C说这观念是赞助人发明来控制艺术家的，课中并无此推论，是topic_adjacent。D说它解释了巴洛克为何回避情感强度，与讲座“情感强烈是回应赞助需求”相反，是reversed_logic。

items.subject: biology
items.topic: the role of bioluminescence in deep-sea organisms
items.transcript: In the deep ocean, sunlight never reaches. Below about a thousand meters, the water is completely dark and cold. Yet this world is not without light. Many creatures here make their own, through a process called bioluminescence. Bioluminescence is the production of light by a living organism. It happens through a chemical reaction. A molecule called luciferin reacts with oxygen, and this releases energy in the form of light. Unlike a light bulb, the reaction gives off almost no heat, so scientists call it cold light. Now, why would an animal spend energy making light in the dark? It turns out bioluminescence serves several purposes. Some animals use it to attract prey. The anglerfish, for example, dangles a glowing lure in front of its mouth, and smaller fish swim toward the light, straight into a trap. Other animals use light for defense. Certain squid release a cloud of glowing fluid to confuse a predator while they escape, much like an octopus releases ink. Light can also help animals find a mate in the darkness, by flashing a pattern that members of the same species recognize. So in an environment with no sunlight at all, life has evolved its own light, and it uses that light in remarkably different ways. What looks like a single ability is really a whole toolkit, shaped by the pressures of survival in the deep.

questions.stem: What is the lecture mainly about?

options.A: Why the deep ocean is colder than the water near the surface
options.B: How the anglerfish hunts smaller fish for its food
options.C: The chemistry of how a household light bulb produces heat
options.D: How deep-sea animals produce and use their own light

questions.explanation: 正确答案D概括主旨：深海动物如何产生并利用自身的光。A只谈深海寒冷这一背景细节，是too_narrow。B只聚焦鮟鱇鱼这一个例子，是too_narrow。C讲灯泡产热，用了“light bulb”这个词却偏离主题，是surface_word_trap。

questions.stem: According to the professor, how is bioluminescent light produced?

options.A: Luciferin reacts with oxygen and releases energy as light
options.B: Sunlight is stored during the day and released at night
options.C: Friction between an animal's scales creates sparks of light
options.D: The animal absorbs light from other glowing creatures

questions.explanation: 正确答案A准确复述：荧光素与氧气反应，以光的形式释放能量。B说是白天储存阳光夜间释放，深海无阳光，与讲座矛盾，是reversed_logic。C说靠鳞片摩擦产生火花，课中无此说法，是topic_adjacent。D说从其他发光生物吸光，改变了关键事实，是wrong_detail。

questions.stem: Why does the professor mention the anglerfish?

options.A: To prove that anglerfish are the most common deep-sea fish
options.B: To give an example of using light to attract prey
options.C: To explain that the anglerfish dangles a glowing lure
options.D: To compare anglerfish with squid that release ink

questions.explanation: 正确答案B：鮟鱇鱼是“用光吸引猎物”这一用途的例子。A说要证明鮟鱇鱼最常见，是教授未表达的over_inference。C只是复述鮟鱇鱼垂下发光诱饵这一内容，而非解释提及它的目的，是literal_restatement。D把目的错认成与乌贼喷墨作比较，弄错了用意，是wrong_purpose。

questions.stem: What does the professor imply about bioluminescence in the deep sea?

options.A: It is used by every deep-sea animal for the same purpose
options.B: It is mainly a way for animals to keep themselves warm
options.C: It serves several different survival functions for different animals
options.D: It works only when some sunlight still reaches the water

questions.explanation: 正确答案C：教授把它称为“工具箱”，说明不同动物把光用于多种生存功能。A说每种动物用途相同，过度概括，与“用途各异”相反，是over_generalization。B说主要用来保暖，但讲座强调这是几乎不产热的冷光，是reversed_logic。D说只有还有阳光时才起作用，但讲座说该处完全无阳光，是topic_adjacent。
